perturbation.original_text: Within 10 days of identifying a Business Source, Monnit shall amend Exhibit A to include such Business Source and deliver a copy of the amended Exhibit A to Receiving Party. The Receiving Party shall have 2 business days to object to the amendment to Exhibit A by providing documentation that evidences Receiving Party's prior relationship with the Business Source added to Exhibit A. If Receiving Party fails to object or provide evidence documenting Receiving Party's prior relationship with the Business Source, the revised Exhibit A shall be deemed automatically amended and incorporated into this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Within 5 days of identifying a Business Source, Monnit shall amend Exhibit A to include such Business Source and deliver a copy of the amended Exhibit A to Receiving Party. The Receiving Party shall have 10 business days to object to the amendment to Exhibit A by providing documentation that evidences Receiving Party's prior relationship with the Business Source added to Exhibit A. If Receiving Party fails to object or provide evidence documenting Receiving Party's prior relationship with the Business Source, the revised Exhibit A shall be deemed automatically amended and incorporated into this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by altering the timeframes for Monnit to amend Exhibit A (from 10 days to 5 days) and for the Receiving Party to object (from 2 days to 10 days). This contradicts the requirement for swift action on both sides, creating uncertainty regarding the timely inclusion of new Business Sources and the objection process.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the timeframes for Monnit to amend Exhibit A (from 10 days to 5 days) and for the Receiving Party to object (from 2 days to 10 days). The contradicted text states 'Within 10 days of identifying a Business Source, Monnit shall amend this Exhibit A to include such Business Source'. The Changed text now states 'Within 5 days of identifying a Business Source, Monnit shall amend this Exhibit A to include such Business Source'. This is a direct contradiction as it changes the timeframe for Monnit to amend Exhibit A. The explanation states 'This change introduces an in-text contradiction by altering the timeframes for Monnit to amend Exhibit A (from 10 days to 5 days) and for the Receiving Party to object (from 2 days to 10 days). This contradicts the requirement for swift action on both sides, creating uncertainty regarding the timely inclusion of new Business Sources and the objection process.' According to the provided definitions, this falls under 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines.

perturbation.original_text: Neither the Receiving Party nor any of its agents shall, in any manner access, contact, solicit or conduct any business with a Business Source that has been made available by and through Monnit. The Receiving Party shall not in any way whatsoever circumvent or attempt to circumvent Monnit and Page 1 of 4 shall not enter into direct or indirect offers, negotiations or transactions with a Business Source revealed by Monnit.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party, after written consent from Monnit, may access, contact, solicit or conduct any business with a Business Source that has been made available by and through Monnit. The Receiving Party shall not in any way whatsoever circumvent or attempt to circumvent Monnit and Page 1 of 4 shall not enter into direct or indirect offers, negotiations or transactions with a Business Source revealed by Monnit.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction by allowing the Receiving Party to engage with Business Sources provided by Monnit, given written consent. This directly contradicts the initial clause that strictly prohibits such engagement. This undermines the non-solicitation clause, and creates uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Neither the Receiving Party nor any of its agents shall, in any manner access, contact, solicit or conduct any business with a Business Source that has been made available by and through Monnit. The Receiving Party shall not in any way whatsoever circumvent or attempt to circumvent Monnit and Page 1 of 4 shall not enter into direct or indirect offers, negotiations or transactions with a Business Source revealed by Monnit.
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows the Receiving Party to engage with Business Sources with written consent from Monnit, directly contradicting the contradicted text which states that "Neither the Receiving Party nor any of its agents shall, in any manner access, contact, solicit or conduct any business with a Business Source that has been made available by and through Monnit". This creates a clear and direct conflict, making the contract unenforceable. According to the Perturbation Explanantion, this undermines the non-solicitation clause and introduces uncertainty.

perturbation.original_text: The parties shall maintain complete confidentiality regarding each other's business and/or their affiliates and shall only disclose information pertaining to the Business Sources as permitted by Monnit, unless agreed and granted an expressed written permission of Monnit.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties shall maintain reasonable confidentiality regarding each other's business and/or their affiliates and shall only disclose information pertaining to the Business Sources as generally understood, unless agreed and granted an expressed written permission of Monnit.
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation replaces 'complete confidentiality' with 'reasonable confidentiality' and 'as permitted by Monnit' with 'as generally understood', creating an in-text contradiction regarding the expected level of confidentiality. This undermines the original stringent requirement, potentially affecting the enforceability of the non-disclosure clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties shall maintain complete confidentiality regarding each other's business and/or their affiliates and shall only disclose information pertaining to the Business Sources as permitted by Monnit, unless agreed and granted an expressed written permission of Monnit.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates 'complete confidentiality' and disclosure only 'as permitted by Monnit.' The changed text alters this to 'reasonable confidentiality' and disclosure 'as generally understood.' The contradicted text states 'complete confidentiality' and disclosure 'as permitted by Monnit.' The change from 'complete' to 'reasonable' directly contradicts the original requirement of 'complete confidentiality' in the contradicted text. The change from 'as permitted by Monnit' to 'as generally understood' also changes the standard and control of what information can be disclosed, creating another contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Compensation shall be negotiated separately and through a subsequent agreement by the Receiving Party and Monnit. Both parties agree that any business transaction(s) between the Receiving Party and those Business Sources provided by Monnit will not be conducted without the prior agreement of Monnit’s compensation for such business transaction(s).
perturbation.changed_text: Compensation may be negotiated separately and through a subsequent agreement by the Receiving Party and Monnit. Both parties agree that any business transaction(s) between the Receiving Party and those Business Sources provided by Monnit will not be conducted without the prior agreement of Monnit’s compensation for such business transaction(s).
perturbation.explanation: Changing "shall be negotiated" to "may be negotiated" creates an in-text contradiction by making the compensation negotiation optional. This undermines the broker relationship and creates uncertainty over whether Monnit is guaranteed compensation for business it facilitates. This contradicts with the subsequent statement that business transactions 'will not be conducted without the prior agreement of Monnit’s compensation'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Compensation shall be negotiated separately and through a subsequent agreement by the Receiving Party and Monnit. Both parties agree that any business transaction(s) between the Receiving Party and those Business Sources provided by Monnit will not be conducted without the prior agreement of Monnit’s compensation for such business transaction(s).
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters 'shall be negotiated' to 'may be negotiated', making compensation negotiation optional. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text, which states that 'Compensation shall be negotiated separately'. The original text and the contradicted text are in the same location, reinforcing the original requirement for mandatory negotiation. The change undermines the certainty of Monnit receiving compensation, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In the event of circumvention by any of the undersigned Parties, whether direct and/or indirect, the circumvented Party shall be entitled to a legal monetary compensation equal to ten times the maximum service fees it would have realized from such a transaction, plus any and all expenses, including any and all legal fees incurred in lieu of the recovery of such compensation.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event of circumvention by any of the undersigned Parties, whether direct and/or indirect, the circumvented Party shall be entitled to a legal monetary compensation equal to ten times the service fees it would have realized from such a transaction, without legal fees incurred in lieu of the recovery of such compensation.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by removing the mention of legal fees incurred in lieu of recovery in the monetary compensation calculation for circumvention. By removing 'any and all expenses, including any and all legal fees incurred in lieu of the recovery of such compensation.' now there is no way to recover the legal fees if the circumverted party needs to hire a lawyer to get the money back.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event of circumvention by any of the undersigned Parties, whether direct and/or indirect, the circumvented Party shall be entitled to a legal monetary compensation equal to ten times the maximum service fees it would have realized from such a transaction, plus any and all expenses, including any and all legal fees incurred in lieu of the recovery of such compensation.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the provision for recovering legal fees incurred in pursuing compensation for circumvention. The contradicted text explicitly states that the circumvented party is entitled to 'any and all expenses, including any and all legal fees incurred in lieu of the recovery of such compensation.' The changed text omits the legal fees, creating a contradiction because it now limits the recoverable expenses in a way that directly conflicts with the original, contradicted clause, making it inconsistent.

perturbation.original_text: This agreement is valid for five (5) years from the date of signature, for any and all transactions between the Parties therein, with renewal to be agreed upon between the signatories.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement is valid for five (5) years from the date of signature, for any and all transactions between the Parties therein, with automatic renewal for one (1) year unless either party objects with a 60 day notice.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction by changing the renewal terms. The original text requires a mutual agreement for renewal, but the change introduces automatic renewal unless objected to. This creates uncertainty about the contract's duration and the conditions for renewal.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This agreement is valid for five (5) years from the date of signature, for any and all transactions between the Parties therein, with renewal to be agreed upon between the signatories.
perturbation.justification: The original text and the contradicted text are the same, stating that renewal requires mutual agreement. The changed text introduces automatic renewal unless objected to. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text because it alters the fundamental condition for renewal from mutual agreement to automatic renewal with an opt-out, creating a clear and direct inconsistency.
